item.title: CADENAS: Three and a Half Years Learning to Be a Software Engineer
item.description: Where two years of self-taught programming became a career - domain depth, automation instincts, and the cost of the wrong CMS decision.

item.title: CircuitMess Subscription API: Killing Duplicate Shipments with a Centralized Order Brain
item.description: Greenfield Node.js + GCP + MySQL subscription API that ended 100+ duplicate shipments for a Forbes-featured STEM toy company.

item.title: Clipster: From MVP on a Single Server to 100 Billion Views
item.description: Backend rewrite from a single-server Svelte MVP to Go + GCP + PostgreSQL - creator payouts, OAuth RFCs, and scale.

item.title: Own Your Corner of the Internet: A Developer's Guide to Permanent Online Presence
item.description: I spent a decade scattering my work across platforms and usernames. Here’s the setup that fixed it: one domain, one blog, everything else is distribution.

item.title: Developers Have Been Dying Since 2003
item.description: Every AI wave was supposed to kill developers. Every wave just raised the floor.

item.title: Shopify's March 2026 Dev Platform Update: What Actually Matters
item.description: RBAC in the Dev Dashboard, a faster Admin API, and safer CLI deploys - the highlights worth knowing.

item.title: Beyond the Badge: What I Learned Passing the Google Cloud Associate Engineer Exam
item.description: The ACE exam tests how you think, not what you memorized. How I prepared, what actually helped, and what the certification means beyond the badge.

item.title: About
item.description:
Hi, I’m Marijan Batarilo

I build systems, then break them.

Over seven years of production backends, e-commerce platforms, cloud infrastructure (GCP), systems under real load. Now doing application security on the same kinds of systems I used to build. A lot of the code shipping today is AI-generated, and it ships fast and breaks worse.

This site is the permanent home for everything I make and write, case studies from real projects, security writeups, learnings and tooling.

item.title: Contact
item.description:
Let’s Connect

You can contact me on LinkedIn or just send me an email.

Code lives on GitHub.
